strengths: Proven backend/distributed-systems expertise with event-driven architectures (Dunnhumby, GISCollective) and low-latency systems (Dlang, asynchronous I/O)
Direct exposure to AI-agent infrastructure development (re:cinq) and tooling for agentic workflows (MCP code generators)
Strong track record scaling engineering teams and practices (RIDE Capital, Altom Consulting) with disciplined methodologies (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Limited explicit fintech experience (credit systems, payment flows) despite general backend and AI-agent familiarity
No documented leadership of 30+ engineers or fintech-scale AI system governance in regulated environments
Recent career gap (Oct 2025–Present) with no context on current technical contributions or readiness
response.justification: Bogdan’s hands-on work with event-driven systems, low-latency architectures, and AI-agent tooling (re:cinq) aligns strongly with the CTO role’s core technical demands. His history of scaling engineering practices and distributed-systems expertise supports rapid, reliable delivery in a fintech context. While fintech-specific experience is lighter and leadership scale less explicit, his demonstrated technical leadership, modular-design instincts, and operational rigor mitigate these gaps. The recent gap warrants discussion but does not invalidate his qualifications for a senior technical leadership role.
